page: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-176-lssd is a high-capacity server instance within the c3 family, powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 8481C processors. It features 176 vCPUs across 88 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a base clock speed of 2.7 GHz. The system is equipped with 704 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4 GB of memory per vCPU. Storage is a major feature of this configuration, which bundles 12,599 GB of local NVMe SSD capacity across 32 local drives.

Benchmark data highlights top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core and Passmark CPU scores ranking in the top 10%. The Intel Xeon architecture includes advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), which drive top-tier results in extended instructions, encryption, and physics simulations. Memory bandwidth is exceptionally high, with cached small-block reads reaching 11,960.2 GB/sec and uncached large-block reads at 445.45 GB/sec. However, single-core performance remains in the average tier, as shown by stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores.

Given its massive local NVMe storage capacity and high memory bandwidth, this instance is highly optimized for storage-intensive and database workloads. It achieves top-tier performance in Redis SET operations (164.22 million operations per second) and static web serving throughput (127.49 GB/sec), alongside strong database operations performance. The presence of AMX instructions also supports strong prompt processing speeds for large language model inference. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the dense integration of 32 local SSDs and high vCPU count offers a consolidated platform for high-throughput database clusters and data-intensive applications, though the average single-core performance suggests it is less suited for single-threaded tasks.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-176-lssd is a high-capacity server powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 8481C processors, featuring 176 vCPUs, 704 GB of RAM, and 12,599 GB of local NVMe SSD storage across 32 drives. It delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance and top-tier memory bandwidth, with cached read speeds reaching 11,960.2 GB/sec. The processor supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX, enabling top-tier results in encryption, physics, and ray tracing. While single-core performance is average, its massive local storage and high-throughput capabilities make it highly efficient for database operations, Redis deployments, and high-volume static web serving. This instance is best suited for data-intensive workloads, large-scale databases, and parallelized computing tasks.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-176-lssd features 176 vCPUs, 704 GB RAM, and 12,599 GB of local NVMe SSD. It delivers top-tier multi-core performance and high memory bandwidth for database workloads.

tagline: A high-core-density server bundling massive local NVMe storage and top-tier multi-core performance for data-intensive and database workloads.
bullet_points: 176 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8481C processors.
12,599 GB of local NVMe SSD storage across 32 drives.
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance.
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction support for advanced workloads.
Optimized for high-throughput databases, Redis, and static web serving.
